observations.recommendation:
5. Prepare and submit the required Bank Reconciliation Statements; and
6. Reclassify and/or adjust transaction accounts that had been already credited in Cash In Bank account but checks issued thereon were cancelled and/or not presented for payment as of December 31, 2020.

9. We recommended that the concerned officials of the Municipality establish and maintain a GAD database containing Sex Disaggregated data and gender statistics and include it in the preparation of the GAD Planning and Budget in compliance with PCW/NEDA/ DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.

1.5 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to duly recognize the monthly depreciation of their PPEs in
order to reflect its correct carrying amounts.

2.5 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant the submission of RCD with official receipts and validated
deposit slips to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period
pursuant to Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

3.5 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to facilitate the preparation and prompt submission of the
monthly BRS to the Audit Team for their review, and as required under
Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and Section 3 of COA Circular No. 96-011. In
case of delayed production of the bank statements, securing a snapshots
as of a given date will do.

4.5 We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the submission of
POs/contract documents not later than five (5) working days from
perfection of contract together with duly accomplished supporting
documents and the AIRs within 24 hours from acceptance of goods, in
compliance with COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006.

5.6 We recommended and Management agreed to require the BAC
Secretariat in collaboration with all concerned key offices to facilitate the
preparation and submission of a comprehensive, workable and realistic
APP pursuant to Section 7 Rule II of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

6.3 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Officer to
prepare and submit the Report on LDRMMF every month, whether
there is transaction or utilization for the period, as required by COA
Circular No. 2012-002.

1. We recommended that Management create an Inventory Committee, composed of a representative from the Mayor’s Office, the General Services Office/or the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and in other departments and conduct the required annual physical count of property, plant and equipment of the Municipality.

2. We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct Head of General Services Office, in coordination with the Municipal Social Worker for Development Office to prepare and submit the RSMI and RIS to the Municipal Accountant to support the credits made to account Welfare Goods for Distribution.

3. We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the separate Trial Balance for the Special accounts in the General Fund, most importantly for development projects funded out of IRA that will facilitate reporting of funds utilized and review thereof.

4. We recommended that Municipal Mayor will assign personnel who will prepare the report and submit its Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation for the AAR 2019 and to strictly comply with the submission of AAPSI in the ensuing annual audit reports.

8. We recommended that Management direct the Accountant to reflect the current portion of Loans Payable separately from its long term debt in the FS, so that its users will be accordingly apprised of the status of its obligation.

1) We recommended that Management initiate the conduct of a complete physical count of the municipality’s properties by creating an Inventory Committee composed of a representative each from the Mayor’s Office, from the General Services Office (or the Municipal Treasurer’s Office) and a representative from each department to prepare the necessary procedures in the proper and orderly conduct of the physical count of the all the property, plant and equipment and inventories owned by the Municipality.
3) We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the required Bank Reconciliation Statements on a monthly basis pursuant to the rules and regulations provided by the Commission.

11) We recommended that management direct the Accountant to reflect the current portion of Loans Payable separately from its long term debt in the FS, so that its users will be accordingly apprised of the status of its obligation.

13) We recommend to initiate the conduct of a complete physical inventory of the municipality’s properties by creating an Inventory Committee composed of a representative each from the Mayor’s Office, from the General Services Office (or the Municipal Treasurer’s Office) and a representative from each department to prepare the necessary procedures in the proper and orderly conduct of the physical count of the all the property, plant and equipment and inventories owned by the Municipality.

14) We recommend that the concerned officials of the Municipality establish and maintain a GAD database containing Sex Disaggregated data and gender statistics and include it in the preparation of the GAD Planning and Budget in compliance with PCW/NEDA/ DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.

1.6 We recommended that Management create an Inventory Committee,
composed of a representative from the Mayor’s Office, the General
Services Office/or the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and in other
departments and conduct the required annual physical count of
property, plant and equipment of the municipality.

2.9 We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Head of General
Services Office, in coordination with the Municipal Social Worker for
Development Office to prepare and submit the RSMI and RIS to the
Municipal Accountant to support the credits made to account Welfare
Goods for Distribution.

3.6 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant
to prepare the separate Trial Balance for the Special accounts in the
General Fund, most importantly for development projects funded out
of IRA that will facilitate reporting of funds utilized and review
thereof.

4.4 We recommended that Management instruct the GAD Focal Person
to prepare and submit the GAD accomplishment report accompanied
with its supporting documents to the Office of the Office of the
Auditor for validation and tracking of the resources on GAD focused
policies, programs and projects based on the approved GAD Plan
pursuant to Joint Circular No. 2004-1.

5.2 We recommended that Municipal Accountant:
a. Prepare and submit the required Bank Reconciliation
Statements; and
b. Reclassify and/or adjust affected transaction accounts that had
been already credited in Cash In Bank account but checks
issued thereon were cancelled and/or not presented for
payment as of December 31, 2020.

6.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor will assign personnel
who will prepare the report and submit its Agency Action Plan and
Status of Implementation for the AAR 2019 and to strictly comply
with the submission of AAPSI in the ensuing annual audit reports.

2) We recommended that the Acting General Services Officer submit the duly filled up driver’s trip tickets, Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption in the use of government vehicles as required in Section 361 of GAAM, Volume I, and to support all purchases of fuel with complete documentation pursuant to Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001.

4) We recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the concerned personnel and require the payee of the subject disbursement vouchers to submit the lacking documentary requirements for evaluation in audit.

5) We recommended that Management instruct the concerned personnel to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the submission of the list of all the Municipality’s projects/programs/activities, including the on-going and those that are to be implemented during the year containing the following information: a. The project name; b. Implementing unit, Office or division if it is not the agency as a whole; c. Brief description of the PPA; d. Contractor or supplier, if any; e. Mode of procurement f. Funding Source g. Cost of approve budget h. Project duration including start and completion date; and i. Location

6) We recommended that Management instruct the GAD Focal Person prepare and submit the GAD accomplishment Report accompanied by its supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor for validation and tracking of GAD focused policies, programs and projects based on the approved GAD Plan pursuant to Joint Circular No. 2004-1.
7) We recommended that Management ensure that the Accountant prepares the Report on LDRRMF every month, within the timeframe prescribed, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.

8) We recommended to adhere strictly to the mandated use of government plates and markings. Direct the vehicle driver to prepare the monthly report of fuel consumption and summary of official travel for proper control and monitoring. Otherwise non-compliance therewith shall cause the disallowance of all expenses such as fuel, oil, gasoline, spare parts and vehicle repair and maintenance pursuant to COA Circular No. 75-6 and 77-61.
9) We recommended to adhere to the above cited COA Circular and submission of the above requirements for verification and analysis to the COA Audit Team before issuance of Disallowance in Audit.

10) We recommended that the management may adhere to the provision of COA Memorandum No. 87 dated July 27, 1987 as amended by COA Memorandum No. 98-016, dated June 16, 1998 for public participation in the promotion of public accountability.

12) We recommend that the Municipal Engineering Office strictly observe the afore-cited regulations to furnish the Office of the Auditor of copies of the Suspension orders for its immediate determination if the grounds for suspension is reasonable or not.
15) Settle the Audit suspensions

3. We recommended that the management may adhere to the provision of COA Memorandum No. 87 dated July 27, 1987 as amended by COA Memorandum No. 98-016, dated June 16, 1998 for public participation in the promotion of public accountability.

4. We recommended that management direct the Accountant to reflect the current portion of Loans Payable separately from its long term debt in the FS, so that its users will be accordingly apprised of the status of its obligation.

5. We recommend that the Municipal Engineering Office strictly observe the afore-cited regulations to furnish the Office of the Auditor copies of the Suspension orders for immediate determination if the grounds for suspension is reasonable or not.

7. We recommend that the concerned officials of the Municipality establish and maintain a GAD database containing Sex-Disaggregated data and include it in the preparation of the GAD Planning and Budget in compliance with PCW/NEDA/ DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.

1.3 We recommended that Management initiate the conduct of a complete
physical count of the municipality’s properties by creating an Inventory
Committee composed of a representative each from the Mayor’s Office,
from the General Services Office (or the Municipal Treasurer’s Office)
and a representative from each department to prepare the necessary

3.4 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit
the required Bank Reconciliation Statements on a monthly basis
pursuant to the rules and regulations provided by the Commission.

4.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the
concerned personnel and require the payee of the subject disbursement
vouchers to submit the lacking documentary requirements for
evaluation in audit.

5.3 We recommended that the concerned officers strictly comply with the
provision of COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the requirement to submit
list of all on- going government projects/programs/activities and those
that are to be implemented during the year containing the following
information:
a. The project name;
b. Implementing unit, Office or division if it is not the agency as a
whole;
c. Brief description of the PPA;
d. Contractor or supplier, if any;
e. Mode of procurement
f. Funding Source
g. Cost of approve budget
h. Project duration including start and completion date; and
i. Location

6.6 We recommended that Management instruct the GAD Focal Person to
prepare and submit the GAD accomplishment report accompanied with
its supporting documents of gender responsive and/or GAD to the
Office of the Auditor for validation and tracking of the resources on
GAD focused policies, programs and projects based on the approved
GAD Plan pursuant to Joint Circular No. 2004-1.

7.3 We recommended that the Municipality ensure that the Accountant
prepares the Report on LDRRMF every month whether there is

2012-002.

1. We recommended to adhere strictly to the mandated use of government plates and markings. Direct the vehicle driver to prepare the monthly report of fuel consumption and summary of official travel for proper control and monitoring.
2. We recommended to adhere to the above cited COA Circular and submission of the above requirements for verification and analysis to the COA Audit Team before issuance of Disallowance in Audit.

6. We recommend to initiate the conduct of a complete physical inventory of the municipality’s properties by creating an Inventory Committee composed of a representative each from the Mayor’s Office, from the General Services Office (or the Municipal Treasurer’s Office) and a representative from each department to prepare the necessary procedures in the proper and orderly conduct of the physical count of the all the property, plant and equipment and inventories owned by the Municipality.

a. Initiate the conduct of a complete physical inventory of the municipality’s properties by creating an Inventory Committee composed of a representative each from the Mayor’s Office, from the General Services Office (or the Municipal Treasurer’s Office) and a representative from each department to prepare the necessary procedures in the proper and orderly conduct of the physical count of the all the property, plant and equipment and inventories owned by the Municipality; and b. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of all the properties recorded in the books and those not yet recorded but actually found in the LGU offices/premises and to prepare the corresponding inventory reports thereof; and reconcile them with the accounting records to come up with the correct account balances in the financial statement; c. Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to maintain stock, property and ledger cards, respectively to properly account the receipt and disposition of property and supplies.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant shall record and post in the Cashbook and the books of accounts respectively the fund transfer amounting to P1,320,000.00 and adjust the cash and cash equivalent accounts so as to present the financial statements of the agency fairly presented and free from material misstatements. Furthermore, strictly observe the procedures and process of collections and deposits of income and revenue.

We recommend that Management strictly require the preparation and approval of the PO before any payment is made on the purchase of materials for projects implemented by the Municipal Government in compliance on the COA Circular.
